methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $3.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $8.6B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Wayve
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $8.6B

private.name: Cohere
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $7B

private.name: Hugging Face
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $4.5B

private.name: Hive AI

private.name: Labelbox
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
ElevenLabs is a Series D AI & Software company seeking valuation guidance against a comp universe of 5 same-stage private peers (Wayve, Cohere, Hugging Face, Hive AI, Labelbox) and 1 public comparable (Palantir). The target raised $500M to date and carries a stated post-money valuation of $3.0B.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement to the $3.0B last post-money, yielding $4.5B. Peer-median analysis across the 5 same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median of $4.5B, with a range spanning $1.0B (Labelbox) to $8.6B (Wayve). The two methods converge exactly at the median.

The triangulated valuation range is $3.8B (low) to $5.2B (high), centered on $4.5B. This implies modest upside from the stated $3.0B valuation, consistent with normal Series D progression. Key uncertainties include ElevenLabs' relative product-market penetration versus Wayve and Cohere (the high-end peers) and relative unit economics versus Hive AI and Labelbox (the low-end). Revenue multiples and customer concentration would refine the range further.
